questions.prompt:
A researcher studies whether a guided breathing routine affects resting heart rate. Group A completes 10 minutes of slow guided breathing each day for two weeks. Group B sits quietly for the same amount of time without breathing instructions. Resting heart rate is measured at the end of the study.
Group | Average resting heart rate (beats/min)
A | 68
B | 75
Which conclusion is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Guided breathing was associated with a lower average resting heart rate.
choices.B: Guided breathing was associated with a higher average resting heart rate.
choices.C: Both groups had exactly the same resting heart rate.
choices.D: Resting heart rate cannot be measured in beats per minute.

questions.correctAnswerText: Guided breathing was associated with a lower average resting heart rate.
questions.explanation: Group A used guided breathing and had a lower average resting heart rate than Group B.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student compares the momentum of four moving objects. Momentum can be calculated as mass × velocity.
Object | Mass (kg) | Velocity (m/s)
A | 3 | 6
B | 5 | 4
C | 8 | 2
D | 2 | 9
Which object has the greatest momentum?

choices.A: Object A
choices.B: Object B
choices.D: Object D

questions.correctAnswerText: Object B
questions.explanation: Momentum = mass × velocity. Object B has 5 kg × 4 m/s = 20 kg·m/s, greater than A = 18, C = 16, and D = 18.

questions.prompt:
A scientist compares two farming methods. In one method, fields are left bare after harvest. In another method, cover crops are planted between growing seasons. Cover crops can add organic matter, reduce soil compaction, and help soil hold nutrients.
Which outcome would best support the use of cover crops?

choices.A: Fields with cover crops show higher soil organic matter after several seasons.
choices.B: Fields with cover crops stop receiving sunlight.
choices.C: Cover crops turn soil into igneous rock.
choices.D: Cover crops prevent all seasonal weather changes.

questions.correctAnswerText: Fields with cover crops show higher soil organic matter after several seasons.
questions.explanation: Cover crops can add organic matter and improve soil quality.

questions.prompt:
A person’s pupils change size in response to light. In bright light, muscles in the iris make the pupil smaller, reducing the amount of light entering the eye. In dim light, the pupil becomes larger so more light can enter.
Which body system coordinates this rapid response?

choices.A: Nervous system
choices.B: Digestive system
choices.D: Excretory system only

questions.correctAnswerText: Nervous system
questions.explanation: The nervous system coordinates rapid responses such as pupil size changes.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A 6 kg object moves at 7 m/s.
Momentum = mass × velocity
What is the object’s momentum in kg·m/s?
Type your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: Momentum = 6 kg × 7 m/s = 42 kg·m/s.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A student tests how the mass of a cart affects its momentum when all carts move at the same speed. The student uses carts of different masses and releases each cart so it reaches 2 m/s.
Which TWO factors should be kept constant?

choices.A: Cart speed
choices.B: Track surface
choices.D: Calculated momentum

questions.correctAnswerText: Cart speed | Track surface
questions.explanation: These factors should stay constant so cart mass is the main variable affecting momentum.

questions.prompt: A person with one changed copy of a gene may not show symptoms, but if that person has children with another carrier, a child may inherit two changed copies. This pattern is consistent with [D1], and the person with one changed copy is called a [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=recessive inheritance; D2=carrier
questions.explanation: A carrier has one changed copy and may pass a recessive allele to children.

questions.prompt:
A student hears a siren from an ambulance as it moves past. The pitch sounds higher while the ambulance approaches and lower after it passes. The sound waves are compressed in front of the moving ambulance and stretched behind it.
Which wave phenomenon is described?

choices.A: Doppler effect
choices.B: Chemical neutralization
choices.D: Chromosome separation

questions.correctAnswerText: Doppler effect
questions.explanation: The Doppler effect changes observed pitch when a sound source moves relative to an observer.

questions.prompt:
Ocean scientists study magnetic patterns in rocks on both sides of a mid-ocean ridge. The patterns form matching stripes. New crust forms at the ridge and moves away over time, recording Earth’s magnetic field as it cools.
Which conclusion is best supported by this evidence?

choices.A: Seafloor spreading occurs at mid-ocean ridges.
choices.B: Ocean crust forms only from compacted shells.
choices.C: Earth’s magnetic field prevents all rock formation.
choices.D: Mid-ocean ridges are caused by river flooding.

questions.correctAnswerText: Seafloor spreading occurs at mid-ocean ridges.
questions.explanation: Matching magnetic stripes on both sides of a ridge support seafloor spreading.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A medical lab measures blood oxygen level before and after a breathing treatment.
Condition | Average blood oxygen saturation (%)
Before treatment | 91
After treatment | 96
Which statement is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Average blood oxygen saturation increased after treatment.
choices.B: Average blood oxygen saturation decreased after treatment.
choices.C: Oxygen saturation stayed exactly the same.
choices.D: The treatment made oxygen disappear from the blood.

questions.correctAnswerText: Average blood oxygen saturation increased after treatment.
questions.explanation: Average oxygen saturation increased from 91% to 96%.

questions.prompt: Match each target with the correct tile.

tiles.A: Type of wheel tread used on the model vehicle
tiles.B: Distance the vehicle travels before stopping
tiles.C: Same starting speed in each trial

questions.correctAnswerText: T1=Type of wheel tread used; T2=Distance the vehicle travels before stopping; T3=Same starting speed in each trial
questions.explanation: Wheel tread is changed, stopping distance is measured, and starting speed is controlled.

questions.prompt:
The gallbladder stores bile made by the liver. After a fatty meal, bile is released into the small intestine. Bile helps break large fat droplets into smaller droplets, making it easier for digestive enzymes to act.
Which statement best describes the role of bile?

choices.A: It helps physically break fat into smaller droplets for digestion.
choices.B: It carries nerve signals across synapses.
choices.D: It changes oxygen into carbon dioxide in the lungs.

questions.correctAnswerText: It helps physically break fat into smaller droplets for digestion.
questions.explanation: Bile breaks large fat droplets into smaller droplets, increasing the surface area available to enzymes.

questions.prompt:
A student rubs two different fabrics together and notices that one fabric clings to the other. Electrons moved from one material to the other, leaving the materials with opposite electric charges.
Which interaction best explains the clinging?

choices.A: Opposite electric charges attract.
choices.B: The fabrics became radioactive elements.
choices.D: Gravity disappeared between the fabrics.

questions.correctAnswerText: Opposite electric charges attract.
questions.explanation: Materials with opposite electric charges can attract each other.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A reforestation project increases tree-covered land from 1,200 hectares to 1,500 hectares.
What is the percent increase in tree-covered land?
Type your answer as a percent rounded to the nearest whole number.
questions.explanation: The increase is 300 hectares. 300 ÷ 1,200 × 100 = 25%.

questions.prompt:
A researcher studies whether resistance training affects bone strength. One group completes supervised resistance training for 12 weeks. A comparison group continues normal daily activity. Both groups have the same bone-strength measurement before and after the study.
Why should both groups be measured before and after the study?

choices.A: To compare changes in bone strength over time.
choices.B: To guarantee that both groups become identical.
choices.C: To prevent the comparison group from being useful.
choices.D: To make bones stop responding to exercise.

questions.correctAnswerText: To compare changes in bone strength over time.
questions.explanation: Measuring before and after allows researchers to compare how bone strength changes in each group.

questions.prompt:
A student tests how the surface material of a slide affects the speed of a block at the bottom. The student uses the same block and same slide height for every trial. The surface material is changed.
What is the independent variable?

choices.A: Surface material of the slide
choices.B: Speed of the block at the bottom
choices.D: Same slide height

questions.correctAnswerText: Surface material of the slide
questions.explanation: The independent variable is the factor intentionally changed by the student.

questions.prompt: A ramp allows a worker to lift a heavy object using less input force over a longer distance. The ramp is an [D1], and it can provide [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=inclined plane; D2=mechanical advantage
questions.explanation: A ramp is an inclined plane that can reduce the input force needed by increasing distance.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A doctor tracks average blood pressure after patients begin a supervised walking program.
Time | Average systolic blood pressure (mm Hg)
Start | 146
4 weeks | 138
8 weeks | 132
12 weeks | 128
Which statement is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Average systolic blood pressure decreased over the 12 weeks.
choices.B: Average systolic blood pressure increased over the 12 weeks.
choices.C: Blood pressure stayed exactly the same.
choices.D: Walking made blood pressure drop to zero.

questions.correctAnswerText: Average systolic blood pressure decreased over the 12 weeks.
questions.explanation: Average systolic pressure decreased from 146 to 128 mm Hg.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student compares the distance a sled travels on four surfaces after the same push.
Surface | Distance traveled (m)
Packed snow | 9.6
Short grass | 4.2
Dry pavement | 2.8
Rubber mat | 1.3
Which surface most likely produced the least friction?

choices.A: Packed snow
choices.B: Short grass
choices.D: Rubber mat

questions.correctAnswerText: Packed snow
questions.explanation: The sled traveled farthest on packed snow, suggesting the least friction among the listed surfaces.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A region tracks average glacier thickness and average summer temperature.
Year | Average glacier thickness (m) | Average summer temperature (°C)
1 | 92 | 10.8
2 | 86 | 11.2
3 | 79 | 11.7
4 | 71 | 12.3
Which TWO statements are best supported by the data?

choices.A: Average glacier thickness decreased from Year 1 to Year 4.
choices.B: Average summer temperature increased from Year 1 to Year 4.
choices.D: Summer temperature was lowest in Year 4.
choices.E: Both measurements stayed exactly the same.

questions.correctAnswerText: Average glacier thickness decreased from Year 1 to Year 4. | Average summer temperature increased from Year 1 to Year 4.
questions.explanation: Glacier thickness decreased from 92 m to 71 m, while summer temperature increased from 10.8°C to 12.3°C.

questions.prompt:
Some hormones are released in pulses rather than at a constant rate. For example, growth hormone may be released in larger pulses during deep sleep. These pulses can signal body tissues to support growth and repair.
Which statement best describes one role of growth hormone?

choices.A: It can signal tissues involved in growth and repair.
choices.B: It forms minerals in igneous rock.
choices.C: It bends sound waves around barriers.
choices.D: It carries oxygen as a red blood cell.

questions.correctAnswerText: It can signal tissues involved in growth and repair.
questions.explanation: Growth hormone can affect tissues involved in growth and repair.

questions.prompt:
A student pushes a heavy cabinet across the floor. The cabinet moves at a constant speed because the applied push is equal in size and opposite in direction to friction.
Which statement best describes the forces on the cabinet?

choices.A: The forces are balanced, so the cabinet moves at constant speed.
choices.B: The forces are unbalanced, so the cabinet must accelerate.
choices.C: Friction acts in the same direction as the push.
choices.D: The push removes the cabinet’s weight.

questions.correctAnswerText: The forces are balanced, so the cabinet moves at constant speed.
questions.explanation: Equal opposite forces result in no acceleration, so motion continues at constant speed.

questions.prompt:
A small rocky object enters Earth’s atmosphere. Friction with air heats its surface, producing a bright streak of light. If part of the object reaches the ground, the remaining piece is called a meteorite.
What is the object called while it is producing a bright streak in the atmosphere?

choices.A: Meteor
choices.B: Aquifer
choices.D: Humus layer

questions.correctAnswerText: Meteor
questions.explanation: A meteor is the bright streak produced when an object enters Earth’s atmosphere.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A clinic reviews 450 records. Of these records, 72 show a documented thyroid condition.
What percent of the records show a documented thyroid condition?
Type your answer as a percent rounded to the nearest whole number.

A student compares how different helmet liners affect peak force during a model impact. The same model head is dropped from the same height with each liner. A sensor records peak force during impact.
Which factor is the dependent variable?

choices.A: Peak force recorded by the sensor
choices.B: Type of helmet liner
choices.D: Same drop height

questions.correctAnswerText: Peak force recorded by the sensor
questions.explanation: The dependent variable is the measured peak force after changing helmet liner type.

questions.prompt:
Mitochondria release usable energy from food molecules during cellular respiration. Muscle cells often contain many mitochondria because muscle contraction requires large amounts of usable energy.
Which statement best describes mitochondria?

choices.A: They help cells release usable energy from food molecules.
choices.B: They store groundwater in underground rock layers.
choices.C: They make chromosomes separate during earthquakes.
choices.D: They reflect sunlight from the Moon.

questions.correctAnswerText: They help cells release usable energy from food molecules.
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are involved in cellular respiration, which releases usable energy.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student tests how a lever’s input distance relates to the output distance of a load.
Trial | Input distance moved (cm) | Output distance moved (cm)
A | 10 | 5
B | 20 | 10
C | 30 | 15
D | 40 | 20
Which statement is best supported by the data?

choices.A: The output distance was half the input distance in each trial.
choices.B: The output distance was twice the input distance in each trial.
choices.C: The output distance stayed the same in every trial.
choices.D: Input distance had no relationship to output distance.

questions.correctAnswerText: The output distance was half the input distance in each trial.
questions.explanation: In each trial, output distance is one-half of input distance.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student compares the average distance of four objects from the Sun.
Object | Average distance from Sun (million km)
A | 108
B | 150
C | 228
D | 778
Which object is farthest from the Sun?

choices.A: Object A
choices.B: Object B
choices.D: Object D

questions.correctAnswerText: Object D
questions.explanation: Object D has the greatest listed distance from the Sun, 778 million km.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A lab measures average muscle recovery time after the same exercise.
Group | Average recovery time (hours)
Stretching routine | 18
No stretching routine | 25
Which claim is best supported by the data?

choices.A: The stretching group had a shorter average recovery time.
choices.B: The no-stretching group had a shorter average recovery time.
choices.C: Both groups had the same average recovery time.
choices.D: Stretching made recovery time impossible to measure.

questions.correctAnswerText: The stretching group had a shorter average recovery time.
questions.explanation: The stretching group averaged 18 hours compared with 25 hours for the no-stretching group.

questions.prompt:
A student uses polarized sunglasses to reduce glare from reflected sunlight. The lenses allow light vibrating in some directions to pass while blocking much of the horizontally reflected light.
Which wave property is being used?

choices.A: Polarization
choices.B: Boiling point
choices.D: Plate tectonics

questions.correctAnswerText: Polarization
questions.explanation: Polarized lenses reduce glare by blocking light vibrating in certain directions.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A researcher studies whether music tempo affects walking pace. Volunteers walk on the same indoor track while listening to slow, medium, or fast tempo music. Walking pace is measured for each condition.
Which TWO steps would improve the investigation?

choices.A: Use the same track for all walking trials.
choices.B: Measure walking pace the same way each time.
choices.C: Change the track surface for each music tempo.
choices.D: Record only the fastest walking pace.
choices.E: Let each volunteer use a different timing method.

questions.correctAnswerText: Use the same track for all walking trials. | Measure walking pace the same way each time.
questions.explanation: Keeping the track and measurement method constant improves the investigation.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A sound wave has a frequency of 250 Hz and a wavelength of 1.4 m.
Wave speed = frequency × wavelength
What is the speed of the wave?
questions.explanation: Wave speed = 250 Hz × 1.4 m = 350 m/s.

questions.prompt:
A forest area is cleared for construction, but a protected strip of native shrubs and soil is left between the construction site and a stream. Over time, the strip supports insects, birds, and small mammals while also reducing sediment movement into the stream.
Which ecological role is best described?

choices.A: Habitat corridor and buffer zone
choices.B: Food chain with one producer and one consumer
choices.D: Combustion of fossil fuels

questions.correctAnswerText: Habitat corridor and buffer zone
questions.explanation: The protected strip provides habitat and helps reduce sediment movement into the stream.

questions.prompt:
A gene contains instructions for a membrane channel protein that helps ions move across a cell membrane. A mutation changes the channel’s shape, so ions move through it less efficiently.
Which statement best explains the effect of the mutation?

choices.A: A gene change can alter a membrane protein and affect ion movement.
choices.B: Gene changes cannot affect proteins.
choices.C: Ion movement occurs only in rocks.
choices.D: Membrane channels are not made from genetic instructions.

questions.correctAnswerText: A gene change can alter a membrane protein and affect ion movement.
questions.explanation: Genes provide instructions for proteins, and mutations can alter protein shape and function.
